title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer found 6 warnings (no blocking/critical issues) in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`: (1) all matrix strategies lack `fail-fast: false`, hiding multi-operator failures; (2) the `SERVICE_REF` yq extraction has no null guard, causing confusing errors; (3) `setup-envtest` and `controller-gen` are unpinned at `@latest`, breaking reproducibility; and (4-6) three additional warnings were truncated. All listed findings are flagged as auto-fixable.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 65d3234 with Claude CLI

## BLOCKING (0)

No findings.

---

## CRITICAL (0)

No findings.

---

## WARNING (6)

### W-001: Missing fail-fast: false on all matrix strategies
**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:56`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: Every matrix job (test, test-integration, e2e-keystone, build-and-push, helm-push) omits fail-fast: false. The default fail-fast: true cancels remaining legs on the first failure, hiding whether multiple operators are broken in a single run.

**Action Required**: Add fail-fast: false to all five strategy: blocks in the workflow file.

### W-002: Missing null guard on yq output for SERVICE_REF
**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: If the key for the matrix operator is absent from source-refs.yaml, yq outputs the literal string 'null'. The downstream git clone --branch null fails with a confusing remote ref error rather than pointing at the missing YAML key. Sibling yq extractions use // [] fallbacks but SERVICE_REF has no guard.

**Action Required**: Add an explicit null/empty check after the yq extraction and exit with a descriptive ::error:: message if SERVICE_REF is null or empty.

### W-003: Unpinned tool versions break CI reproducibility
**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: setup-envtest and controller-gen are installed at @latest in the test-integration and verify-codegen jobs respectively. A breaking release in either tool will silently start failing CI without any diff to review. All other tooling in the workflow uses pinned versions.

**Action Required**: Pin both tools to specific versions (e.g. controller-gen@v0.17.3, setup-envtest@v0.20.4) and define those version constants in a top-level env: block or Makefile variable for single-source-of-truth maintenance.

### W-004: setup-envtest use without a Kubernetes version
**File**: `Makefile:211`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: setup-envtest use without a version argument downloads the latest supported Kubernetes API server binaries. Different CI runs may download different k8s versions if the tool's concept of 'latest' changes between runs, making integration tests non-deterministic.

**Action Required**: Pin to a specific Kubernetes version, e.g. setup-envtest use 1.32 -p path.

### W-005: internal/common integration tests excluded from CI
**File**: `Makefile:211`
**Actionability**: needs-discussion
**Fix**: ASK

**Problem**: Nine integration test files in internal/common/ use //go:build integration but the test-integration Makefile target only iterates over $(OPERATORS) and runs ./operators/$$op/.... The internal/common path is never passed to go test -tags=integration. This is the shared library underpinning all operators, and its integration coverage is never uploaded despite a codecov 80% target for internal/common/**.

**Action Required**: Add a separate step or dedicated test-integration-common target that runs go test -tags=integration -coverprofile=cover-integration-common.out ./internal/common/... and upload it under an integration-common flag in CI.

### W-006: Mutable latest tag pushed unconditionally on every v* tag build
**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: type=raw,value=latest fires unconditionally in the build-and-push job, both on main branch pushes and on any v* tag push. A v* tag created from a non-main branch commit would overwrite latest with non-main content without any visible signal.

**Action Required**: Restrict the latest tag to the default branch only using type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}.

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: cover-unit-common.out uploaded twice under different Codecov flags
**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
**Actionability**: needs-discussion
**Fix**: ASK

**Problem**: Both matrix legs (keystone and c5c3) run make test, which includes ./internal/common/... and generates cover-unit-common.out. Both legs upload this file to Codecov — once under unit-keystone and once under unit-c5c3. The common component receives duplicate data attributed to operator-specific flags. This will become increasingly confusing as more operators are added.

**Action Required**: Introduce a dedicated unit-common matrix leg or a separate CI step that runs common tests and uploads under a unit-common flag, matching the [common, keystone, c5c3] matrix design from the PR spec.

### I-002: Implementation matrix differs from PR spec
**File**: ``
**Actionability**: needs-discussion
**Fix**: ASK

**Problem**: The PR description specifies refactoring the test job into a matrix over [common, keystone, c5c3]. The implementation uses [keystone, c5c3] with common bundled inside each leg. Common is tested (no functional gap), but the shape diverges from the documented design and causes the duplicate-upload issue described in I-001.

**Action Required**: Either update the PR description to reflect the implemented design, or adjust the matrix to match the spec by extracting common into its own leg.

---

## Summary

| Category  | Count |
|-----------|-------|
| BLOCKING  | 0     |
| CRITICAL  | 0     |
| WARNING   | 6     |
| INFO      | 2     |

**Recommendation**: The PR can be merged with the WARNING findings addressed first. The two highest-priority items before merge are W-005 (internal/common integration tests silently excluded from CI) and W-003 (unpinned tool versions). The remaining warnings are straightforward config fixes that can be applied without discussion. The two INFO findings can be resolved in a follow-up PR focused on Codecov flag hygiene and aligning the implementation with the PR spec.
